name: Gratin of Potatoes, Green Garlic and Thyme
recipeIngredient: 6 large waxy boiling potatoes, sliced 1/4 inch thick
2 heads and stalks green garlic, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine potatoes and garlic in a wide, deep saucepan.
2. Add salt, thyme, and milk to cover the ingredients.
3. Bring mixture to boil over medium heat, then reduce heat and let it simmer until the potatoes are fairly soft, about 30 minutes.
4. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
5. Lightly butter a heavy gratin dish.
6. Starting with the smallest potato pieces or those that have broken, cover the bottom of the dish with potatoes.
7. Layer the remaining potatoes in an overlapping pattern over top, not more than 1 inch deep.
8. Pour just enough milk over to moisten well and dot with butter.
9. Bake at 350°F for 1 hour or until well browned and crusty.
Each serving contains about: 233 calories; 420 milligrams sodium; 25 milligrams cholesterol; 8 grams fat; 34 grams carbohydrates; 7 grams protein; 0.48 gram fiber.
